AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:


Facing competitive pressure from Comcast and dealing with administrative changes to the BEAD program.


The business had completed and submitted BEAD (Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment) program requirements, but administrative changes have invalidated prior submissions, requiring a full reapplication. Additionally, Comcast's lobbying influence and aggressive infrastructure expansion are drawing customers away.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

  • Reapply for BEAD under the new administration guidelines as soon as possible.
  • Begin proactive retention strategies for staff, including competitive compensation and clear role advancement.


NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:

  • Schedule a planning meeting with the new marketing hire.
  • Track areas where Comcast is expanding and assess
Reviewed Customer Avatar Doc. Ordered SEO will set up with DeSia.

Media.Other: null - Family member has used services in Georgia.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: He has HR issues and wants to create a fixed location...Corunna and Court.

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: He needs to hire 2-3 new people. He is using Square and QB now in combination. He has done some outreach to friends etc, and we discussed other sources...various higher ed. He may put together an FB announcement. Pays $12-13/hr. His brother still works with him. He would like to put 2-3 new food concepts together at the former pizza place on Corunna. He would do fries, juices, wings, and fingers here. He has 4 people working now.

 

NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS: He will draft a FB ad

Notes:

  • Met with client for an active session on business planning for his restaurant. 
  • Client obtained equipment quotes totaling approximately $41K; exploring options to reduce cost using lightly used equipment. 
  • Discussed the 30/30/30/10 rule for restaurant financials and how to calculate sales based on cover turnover. 
  • Client will continue working on the business plan. 
  • Next meeting scheduled in about a week.
Zoom call with EdTEch Accelerator team to review companies and pitches and select top 5 to participate in the following week's pitch competition.

Worked on the financial projections and then began sharing how to write the business plan using a business plan template. I sent the below email with both of the documents we worked on.


Plan: They will review the financial projections, make sure they are good with the final document. Once they finalize the financial projections, they will begin writing the draft business plan. They plan to email me the draft plan by Wednesday, June 25th. I will review once I receive it. Our next meeting is scheduled for Jul1 1st from 9-10:30am.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
LivePlan Walkthrough


I met with Brent and Ashley to give a walkthrough of the LivePlan Program. I gave an extensive explanation of the business planning process, what is needed, what is expected and how to come up with the information. I broke down each section explaining the purpose, what is expected and how to complete in LivePlan.


I explained to Ashely how LivePlan has built in tools to assist with her BP creation such as, built in Artificial Intelligence to assist with the "writing", walkthrough videos and direct communication with her consultant.


When we reached the financials portion, I gave a light explanation over the program and with Brent's assistance we explained what is expected and how to complete using a variety of tools.


Ashley will follow up with Brent for general consulting, but I instructed her that I am available for any LivePlan troubleshooting.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
EDO UPDATES

Rita Hillman <rhillma3@emich.edu

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Rob is ready to scale and wants to increase workload to maximize machine usage and efficiency by running operations 24/7. He needs strategic support to structure growth effectively and maintain profitability.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

  • Enroll Rob in the Focus Four program to help prioritize goals and structure his growth strategy.
  • Evaluate job sourcing strategies to keep machines operational around the clock.
  • Review his P&L report to identify areas for margin improvement or reinvestment.
  • Introduce Rob to tools and opportunities related to AI or automation to further enhance productivity.


NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:

  • Shannon to send the Generative AI Guidebook.
  • Rob to send his Profit & Loss report for review.
  • Schedule a follow-up meeting to review P&L and al
Helped the client file for her LLC to officially launch her new business model. Will follow up with Erin to take as a new business start. Need to obtain EIN after filing LLC.

-Wants to buy a business in the existing location from the existing owner in the church. Currently, pays $400 to house the business in St. Peter's Church

-New line of business would include offering pottery class, and selling pottery, will hire interns to create pottery

  • Will need to setup for sales tax license

-Would like to setup pottery sales as a part of the business strategy to earn revenue; she's familiar with a similiar business model in Petosky, MI and would like to duplicate.

-Other business growth ideas; teaching pottery classes; also hire interns who can help teach


Marketing Resources

-Often times the pottery courses around SE Michigan are full; other pottery establishments have agreed to send her customer referrals.

-Other ideas; host Emerging Artists galleries - this could result in wholesale sales; the artist would be 1099 contractors and responsible for collecting sales tax from their customers.</

It ahs been a while since we last spoke. You were thinking you would like to close the business or try to sell it. I wanted to follow up and see if there is anything I can do to help with the process. If you have decided to keep at it, I can help with that too. My calendar link is below if you need it.

Several emails with Ashley to set up meeting. Review file from last year. Zoom meeting today. Online research after the meeting.


The veterinary practice has 10 employees including Ashley, her husband and a day care provider/personal assistant (who takes care of Ashley's 5 children so that both Ashley and her husband can work). Both Ashley and her husband, Dr. Miller, are licensed veterinarians, only Dr Miller is generating revenue. Ashley is managing the business. Ashley hired an office manager about 7 months ago to free Ashley up to practice veterinary medicine, but the hired office manager is not working out and Ashley is still managing the office. Ashley will most likely terminate her next week. Ashley also plans to make both receptionists part-time, one of them is currently full-time. Ashley is moving the bookkeeping/accounting work back to Susan at Crossroads Business Services effective July 1. Financials are on a cash basis. The company has ve

Lisa and her husband live on the family farm run by her brother-in-law. That farm grows organic soybeans and other commodity crops.


They have started experimenting with growing organic sweet potatoes. She had a lot of questions about getting started, getting her organic certification, logo creation and more.


The main issue I saw was that they weren't quite sure where to sell. They were thinking about setting up at the farmer's market this year but would eventually like to offer the potatoes to other outlets such as restaurants.


We talked about a business model canvas as a way to narrow down what product to offer. We also looked at different agencies she may need to work with while getting this business going. She was hoping for some USDA grants, and I was able to look up local resources for the USDA she could speak with.

My email:


Following communications with each of you, I am pleased to make this introduction. I believe your two startups are in synergistic and non-competing areas of solar energy and clean power for off-grid applications. 


Nikunj,

I provided a brief overview of what you are working on to Dean (recently hired CEO) on the phone this afternoon. I think there could be some interesting product and use case/market collaboration opportunities. And as we discussed earlier this week, I think Mojtaba and Dean could provide valuable peer-to-peer / founder-to-founder advice as you are at an earlier stage of company development. Dean has had

We met with Chakor for bubble development detailed planning. i4iq is planning to submit an application for the software pilot, as they are nearing the Customer Discovery criteria for it, and finalizing the Executive Summary. They are planning to preprocess data into their own database, and use the Bubble app to gain traction in preparation for Series A. After the meeting, the founder Scott is intending to put in personal funds of $500 to get started, and has been cautioned that there is no guarantee that the review committee will approve the BAF funding, and in no case will he be reimbursed for that $500 from SBDC, but he has decided to go ahead on his own dime. We analyzed the intended application functionality and Bubble compatibility, timelines, process, logistics and milestones.

1:1 consultation and updates on business progress. Discussed her ongoing efforts to develop a program aimed at supporting young adults aged 18 to 25, particularly focusing on post-high school individuals. 


Victoria expressed a deep passion for creating a space that fosters unity and open dialogue, addressing issues like self-esteem and societal pressures across different racial and socioeconomic backgrounds. She highlighted her personal experiences and a desire to help middle and upper-class youth as well, emphasizing unity as a solution to many underlying problems. 


Key challenges identified include a lack of immediate financial resources to launch her initiative fully, the need to connect with relevant organizations, and the complexity of navigating sensitive cultural and racial discussions.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Consolidate notes from Wednesday meeting. Online research: The Local Credit Union, the BIG I Michigan (trade association representing independent insurance agents and brokers), Vertical IQ. Email notes to Brian Edwards with question.

Hi David,

It was great meeting with you last week — I appreciate you sharing your progress and business plans. It sounds like you’re on a strong path with your live haul tank manufacturing!

Here’s a quick recap of what we discussed, along with some answers to your questions:

  • LLC and Taxation: As a single-member LLC, you’re automatically taxed as a sole proprietorship, meaning business income passes through to your personal tax return. That’s often the simplest option. If your income grows or you want different benefits, you could elect S Corp taxation — we can talk more about when that might make sense.
  • Paying Yourself: With an LLC taxed as a sole prop, you don’t pay yourself a salary — you take an “owner’s draw” from the business account to your personal account. Just be sure to keep records and separate accounts.
  • Should You Stay an LLC? LLCs are flexible and generally a good fit for liability

Client was a no-call no-show for today's meeting. I sent him the following email:


Hi Robbie,


I'm sorry you were unavailable for our meeting. Please be aware that I do organize my week around our meetings, so no-shows and last minute rescheduling is disruptive. Please try your best to attend the appointments you book and I will do the same for you. Also, in the rare cases that you do need to re-schedule with less than a week's notice, please send me a text message so I know right away and have time to find other uses for the time slot.


On a lighter note, congratulations on being accepted to the 5x5 event at Start Garden. I would be happy to help you with your pitch. Please use my calendly link below to schedule a time. Thank you and I look forward to connecting soon, 


Scott

AddressPart1.Street1: 2209 Longyear Ave Apt D, Apt D

AddressPart3.Street1: 2209 Longyear Ave Apt D, Apt D

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Michael called office line while Lance on vacation. Questions regarding hiring son as first employee, what steps to take. Advised him to start account with payroll system like QBO or Gusto. He already uses QB for his business. Business name he gave is ABC Carpentry and Construction, but LARA site down so can't verify. Sent him link to QB payroll. He will start account and get back with me with additional questions during setup. Advised him that YESA, UIA, workers comp will all be required if he hires non family member.

Client asked me to review their proposal that they are submitting to a large potential customer. I suggested that Jason run each of his draft RFP responses through an AI to polish and strengthen them. I shared an example of how to structure the prompts, including background info, the RFP, and his draft response for each bullet point, asking ChatGPT to make it sound professional, exciting, and convincing. I also recommended improving the visual design of the proposal and including key sections like a summary, background, solution overview, timeline, budget, team qualifications, values, and terms. I let him know that using an AI would speed things up and likely lead to a big improvement.

Received notice from Lead Center about email that came in regarding a survey for the theatre their bookkeeper was completing. Lead Center forwarded to me as I'm listed as lead consultant. I responded to the bookkeeper with a follow up email. Following is the original email and my response.


From: Sandra Deschamps <sandy@sawpc.com>

Date: Friday, June 13, 2025 at 10:11 PM

To:

Hi Tishawn,

Great catching up with you last week! It’s exciting to hear you’ve registered your LLC and found a solid vehicle option with the 2017 Chevy Silverado 2500LT. Sounds like you’ve got a strong grasp on your next steps.

As discussed, I’m attaching a one-year income and expense spreadsheet to help you get started on your projections. This will be key when connecting with lenders. I’ll send you a list of lending contacts next week to keep that momentum going.

In the meantime, if you’d like to start exploring grants, here’s a good SBA link with updated info:

🔗 https://www.sba.gov/funding-programs/grants

I’ll also follow up soon with additional insights from a senior consultant regarding your truck financing question.

Let me know how things are going on your end, and feel free to book a time with me here if you'd like to meet aga

Media.Other: I found you on social media

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Summary: the client wrote that he is proceeding with the D2P2 proposal but has hit a snag because he never got a sponsor. As the call states: “It must have validated the product-mission fit between the proposed solution and a potential Air Force and/or Space Force stakeholder. The applicant should have defined a clear, immediately actionable plan with the proposed solution and the DAF customer and end-user. The feasibility study should have:

1. Clearly identified the potential stakeholders of the adapted solution for solving the Air Force and/or Space Force need(s).

2. Described the pathway to integrating with DAF operations, to include how the applicant plans to accomplish core technology development, navigate applicable regulatory processes, and integrate with other relevant systems and/or processes.” He asked if I think this can be fixed in the next week and if I have any recommendations.


Action: I responded, yes and no. Ideally they would have found

Hi David,

It was great connecting last week! You’re making great progress with the business — identifying the Kiovend TSO60 machine from Kiosoft and securing most of your startup funds is a solid start.

I think reaching out to your friend in the gas station business is a great idea to learn more about suppliers and get a better sense of restocking costs. As promised, in our last meeting, I shared vendor contacts from John Schmitt to help expand your sourcing options — let me know if you’d like help reaching out or comparing suppliers.

Once you have a better idea of inventory costs, we can work together to outline your pricing and more.

If you'd like to schedule a follow-up session, feel free to book a time here:

ClientIntake.BusinessType: Real Estate and Rental and Leasing

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi Jasmine,

It was great catching up last week. Thanks again for sharing updates on your development — that upcoming South Haven/Covert Township meeting sounds like a key moment. Please let me know how it goes!

It’s also great that you’re already working with a realtor — they’ll be a great resource for your more specific real estate questions.

As for your idea of an HOA fee, you're on the right track — that can be an effective way to protect property value over time and ensure neighborhood upkeep. For long-term income after the property sells, you might also consider retaining ownership of certain shared amenities or building a small rental portfolio within the development, depending on your goals.

Feel free to book a time here when you're ready to continue:

Summary/Action: The last section that I had to review was for the Company/Team. At this point it reads more like a draft so I am not sure it is ready for review. But my general comments still stand – put it in the same format as outlined in the solicitation. For this section, it will look like: Company and Team Company and Motivation Give the background of the company (when formed, what kind of company (LLC, C-Corp, S-Corp). Explain the motivation for the company in proposing this project. Team Provide a concise description of the relevant qualifications, experience, and expertise of the company founders and the Senior/Key Personnel on the proposed project. To keep this brief, I recommend the following (put the PI first): Name, Title on Project/in Company (Highest Degree, Field, University), will do ….role in project…. Concisely list anything specific to that role. Remember that they will see full resume in the other part of the document so keep this to only special qualifications d
Wanted to send this link your way, there's a manufacturing grant in there. https://www.sba.gov/funding-programs/grants

Summary/Action: The cient and I talked by phone to go over a couple of questions he had about the template that I sent last night as well as the tone of what they are trying to write. We went through a couple of scenerios, but I stressed following the template and NOT relying on ChatGBT for the format of the document because it will get it wrong.


Plan: He will go back through and do the correct format and flesh out the information as necessary for the AF submission.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: While visiting another client in the area, I stopped in the shop to see if Star was in. Unfortunately, she is out due to a recent surgery. I took a look at the shop, and if looks like some changes have been made since our last visit. I will follow up shortly with a message to Star once she is back on her feet.

Media.Other: I found your website on a search engine

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
I met with Brooklyn and Amy Jo at the shop. Unfortunately, Jenna was not able to join us. We had a good conversation, mainly about marketing concerns.

  • Updates: Alterations are going great; Jenna has been really busy with alterations all spring. Sales are slow, but the shop's modest/classic dresses are not for everyone. They also admit that being in a rural town means that the bridal pool is not the biggest. However, they are focused on customer discovery right now, and hope to make long-term investments in the community for future seasons. They are only open a couple of days per week, so the overhead costs are not too much to bear right now.
  • Social Media: Brooklyn has largely taken over social media management. The posts are great - very on-trend, and getting lots of views and interactions across platforms. Brooklyn can only get out 1-2 posts per week with her other work outside of the shop. I don't necessarily think that's an issue, as quality over

Checking in. Will reopen if client responds and keeps appointment.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Good morning, Kristi as promised, I wanted to follow up with you to see if you gained any more information from working with the banks regarding funding. Additionally, here is what I have found that may be helpful to you:

1. Amber Grants for Women

  • Type: Grant
  • Eligibility: Women entrepreneurs
  • Amount: $10,000 monthly grants; eligible for $25,000 year-end grant
  • When: Rolling applications throughout the year
  • Notes: Easy application; highly recommended for female founders.

2. Kalamazoo Small Business Loan Fund

  • Type: SBA Loans & State/Federal Loan Assistance
  • Eligibility: Focus on minority and women-owned businesses in food/bev, retail, manufacturing, or service
  • Amount: $5,000–$50,000
  • When: Open year-round</

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Client has little saved, and poor credit score and wants start up funding to become licensed to sell firearms.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

Discussed the priority of getting his credit score up and the the effect it will have with the business and vendors. Discussed the possibility of selling firearm parts and accessories in stead of weapons. They are more profitable and have fewer licensing reguirements and don't require a store front. Given his situation the parts and accessories alow for a more flexiable start and he can build the business incrementially.

**Finalizing business plan for SBSH GRant. **

Went over last bits of financials for business plan SBSH grant.

Will finalize tomorrow

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Brewery now on hold with food offering. Something happened with Brewery and client didn't pry. Means client needs to find a new location and a perhaps buy their own truck. Changes things dramatically as previous deal included the rental of the truck and much of the food cost upfront. Client would have shared the revenue and Brewery would pay sales tax since they'd collect it. We worked through the EIN details but couldn't completed (she may have done so already?). Walked through 36-month projections again and gave guidance on what to change/explore. Joseph Guenther

Hi Gloria,


Nice to hear from you. I hope your summer is off to a great start!


I read through your pitch and I have several questions. First, who is Freddie Jones? If Metro PCS was going to engage in a plan to distribute cellphones through an outside provider, their National Strategy team would have built a plan for it and seek contracted companies to help. If they don’t have this strategy, reaching out to a customer service representative will not get you anywhere.


Secondly, the pitch mentions commission based sales. Commission on what? If you are asking Metro PCS for free phones to distribute, what would the commission be based off of and what is the percentage?


Lastly, the plan mentions hiring 100 people but with only $50K in sales, how are you going to pay them?


Overall the plan is well written, but I don’t think you can create a need for Metro PCS or any other carrier that isn’t there. All Cell

The System for Award Management (SAM.gov) checks multiple databases to verify if an entity is registered and eligible for federal contracts or assistance. Here are the key databases and systems it integrates with:

 

Databases SAM.gov Checks During Registration or Validation:

  1. FAPIIS (Federal Awardee Performance and Integrity Information System)
    • Checks for performance and integrity information on federal contractors, including terminations and misconduct.
  1. Excluded Parties List System (EPLS) → Now part of SAM.gov

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Email communication with Dave Serino about the $4.5 million valuation. Receipt and review of the full valuation report. It values 100% of the ownership on a stock sale or all-asset sale. The company is extremely profitable with over $800,000 in cash on the balance sheet at 12/31/24. Valuation at 12/31/24 of $4.5 million. The valuation is based on a combination of Income approach (using the capitalization of earnings method at a required rate of return of 17.88% minus 5%for the sustainable growth) equal to $4,090,000; and a market approach: Net Sales x.73 (40% weight), EBITDA x 5.5 and SDE x 3.5. The valuation determined that $639,000 of the cash on the balance sheet at 12/31/24 was "excess cash" so the $639,000 was added to the to the $3.9 million value to get to $4.5 million.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

I met with Kevin to go over the first version of financial projections that I sent him last week. With how his current projections look, his expenses vastly exceeded his income. We worked through each line item to see what adjustments could be made. This included adding in the rental income from his tenants, adding his owner’s withdrawal, cutting down the loan amount by about $40k, and recalculating his expenses. Once we finished, we were a lot closer to being fiscally solvent but still had issues with the expenses.

I spoke with our client by telephone who is in the process of purchasing Stu's Electric in Mt Pleasant. Ben is supposed to take over the company's operations on June 30 as the first day. Their is very little time to study and prepare analysis with what information is available. He shared historical profit & loss statements and balance sheets from the seller for the years ending 12/31 for 2023, 2024 and 5 months year to date for 2025. It is not a lot to go on but I shared my analysis with him:

  • Declining revenues especially for 2025 - Owner had announced he was closing shop and sales fell off. He has since put it up for sale and informed his clientele whereby sales have rebounded and now our client is pursuing purchase of the inventory.
  • Declined margins for 2025 - Owner is selling off stale inventory at a steep discount to clean up and bring the inventory current
  • What are these expenses related to?
    • Job Supplies - sounds like a CoGS -

Introductory Meeting

I met with Bo to discuss his plans to start a Asian Fusion Brewery in Tawas City, MI. Bo has owned and operated a bar/resturant in China for 5 years so he needs some assistance with the US permitting and structure. Bo had some clerical questions over location. Bo has 2 locations in mind:


  1. Former Golf Course
    1. Equipped with
      1. Full Service Kitchen
      2. Liquor License
    2. 3 acre property
    3. $360,000
  2. Former Chinese Restaurant
    1. Successful business pre-covid
    2. Lakefront Property
    3. $910,000

Bo was leaning towards the golf course because of the price and there is development around the perimeter of the property for housing. Bo has already spoken to the bank and they are reluctant to offer a loan to an individual to purchase the property along with the startup process for a restaurant. Bo is going to approach the bank to look into getti

I met with Dustin to get an update on how things are going. He shared that they have made progress with improvements and are finishing the installation of all electrical underground.


They have had numerous problems with teh overhead lines because of all of the trees on the property.


I explained that a new grant program will be available soon and that they should consider a project.


Dustin shared that they are planning to work on the common space and upgraded to a laundry unit for the residents.


I told him that I would notify him as soon as I had information about the grant.


We will plan to get together in the near future to review last year and their plans for the season.

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:


Phone call with Samantha to provide feedback and next steps. Her business plan is lacking detail, it's very high level and written by AI. When I tried providing feedback she got very defensive and became frustrated with me. She was crying and freaking out about not meeting my standards.


Every time I tried speaking she cut me off with some sort of explanation. She said she was expecting tools to do this for her.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

I explained that it is her business plan, I am only here to provide free help to prepare her for funding. That I understand what a lender is looking for.


I also explained that we have tools available to her but that we don't do the work for her.

She requested an in person meeting. I put together resources on industry data, projections

Summarize this email

Matthew's c

ompany is Round Here and they are a marketing resource company that partners with organizations like Tech Town, Bamboo, and New Lab to provide a central location to get information regarding business topics. Their revenue stream is a commission model and they earn 20% of the revenue that is generated by individuals who attend paid events. The platform also allows businesses to work directly with an industry expert to help scale and solve a particular business need.


The company's platform provides a centralized place that business owners can find out about and attend events throughout the State. To date revenues have been $250,000 and profit has been $50,000.


He is in need of: Customer Discovery; developing a pricing model; and coming up with cash flow projections.


Matthew informed me he was referred to Tech Consultant Nancy Benovich for assistance, and I have sent her an email to determine what next st

Asch does not have a great handle on business concepts, but has done a ton of research on MDARD liscencing! I reccommended we make a mini business plan because she has so many expansion ideas but doesn't know where to go. Has a possible food truck idea, and also wants to open up in a kiosk in the mall. They said it was free and they would not have to pay rent but I was unsure how that would work legally speaking. She will be getting in touch with the landlord asap. She will have an investor if she starts making coffee so she is figuring out if that will work or not.

Message From Marc:


Hey Nate everything has been going well. I did have a few questions. Is a certificate of good standing with the state required or recommended? I also got something regarding Osha, I dont have the document with me but I can get the name once I'm home. I believe it was 300 some odd dollars for a safety book, do you know if that's also required? I'm am also shopping around for insurance so I'm wondering what types you would recommend.


My Reply:


Marc,


Glad to hear everything is going well. 


You are NOT required to get a certificate of good standing unless you are doing government contracting. You can do this through LARA for $10 if needed later.


I would assume that the OSHA "Safety Book" is not required for your business so hold off on paying for that. I'm trying to confirm with a colleague that this is true. But my initi

Daniel Yoder <dayoder@mtu.edu>

2:37 PM (0 minutes ago)

to Matthew, Kevin, Ross


Hi Matt,


You are not being a pest at all! We all want the same thing--to see John succeed. 


Early in June, we met with John a couple times, reviewed various sets of John's financials, and helped him create a 3 year financial forecast to show what his business could look like if he were operating in a comparative way to industry norms. We provided him with industry financial benchmarks and a US industry report. We discussed what disconnect may exist between the industry norms and his shop's performance. We offered suggestions on some key focus areas of pricing and marketing.


From my review, if things were going well, I estimated that he could reduce his floor plan balance by about $150K per year if he were able to operate closer to industry averages.

Our client sent us historical information on Stu's Electric, the company he is purchasing. We received:

  • Monthly P&L's for 2022, 2023, 2024
  • Bill payments for 2023 and 2024
  • Statements of Cash Flow for 2022, 2023, 2024, and through June 24th, 2025
  • Bills & Applied Payments for several years

I had to work with the financials to make them readable and put into comparison form. Most useful was the sales information from the monthly P&L's for analyzing seasonality. He indicated that the period prior to the start of heating season was higher in volume. There is some variation month to month but the winter months have the higher sales.

Hi Charles,

Thanks for the update — congrats on getting your LLC formed!

To answer your question: there is no fee to get an EIN from the IRS. It’s completely free, so you should not pay any third-party services for it. If you received the application already, you're almost there — just be sure it's submitted through the official IRS website.

I'll give you a call tomorrow at 3:35 PM. Looking forward to catching up and helping you move forward.

Sincerely,

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Phone call with Nick after he emailed our office. Lives in WA, moving to Detroit soon. I updated his address in Salesforce and updated his region. Has access to 100-300K in cash, wants to buy a business up to $3 million in purchase price, says he is pre-approved by SBA for up to $3million, but doesn't actually have business to buy identified. Recommended at this stage reading resources on buying business, connecting with local to Detroit SBDC consultant, perusing brokerage listings at BizBuySell and Sunbelt, developing relationships with local accountants who may know of businesses selling privately. Told him we can help with financial due diligence and buying process. Emailed him resources after the call and copied Seth and Maia.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Ciarra seemingly needed to get my opinion on what to do. We created a decision tree. She is not willing to rebuild the business and make a go at it. She would consider getting a job and paying $500/mo for 28 years. But her best option, according to her, is bankruptcy. She has tried before with Jeffery Alandt from Traverse City, and he was inept, she said. She's going to look at attorneys in Gaylord. She understands a great deal about the process, and has had a false start. She has done the counseling before.


So, she will circle back with questions and keep me informed on the process and how it goes. No next steps.

Follow up meeting with Joaquin. We reviewed his business plan and worked on the financial projections. He needs funding and has several veteran-eligible grants that he is going to look into and I also sent him Alyssa’s contact info from Huntington for the Lift Local program. I recommended he look into the grants first and if the award period is timely and he can get it then great. Otherwise he can apply for the loan to get funds quicker and pay it off with a grant later or over time with regular payments. He will consider his options and reach out for guidance as needed. We will reconnect to take next steps accordingly but he has everything he needs at this point to talk to the bank.


Sent:

-         Alyssa’s contact information

**CONSULTATION**


Met with Malvika to catch up on her business activities. She recently sold at Vintage in the Zoo and had 30 purchase interactions, noting most buyers were young women. We discussed the importance of tailoring her marketing efforts toward that demographic. She felt confident in her pricing. She’s actively researching new event opportunities and is considering Karma House (Battle Creek), Reits Flea Market, and an Intentional Yoga + Lean Body Studio event. I recommended the Kalamazoo Night Market and will send her the application link. She shared that clothing items drew the most interest. She also plans to follow up with Amanda Binder regarding export opportunities, and I will send her Amanda’s booking link. I’ll also be sending her the SBDC impact form.

Discouraged - Client is getting discouraged that his past efforts haven't worked to grow his classes.


We talked about his upcoming events to spread awareness of the classes he offers. I also shared the idea that he might want to teach kids so he could build his following overtime as they get older. Nathaniel claims he's out of his comfort level with little kids. I might suggest he pair with a youth teacher to work on those skills.


He needs a game plan for more in person events for after the Medieval Fair in early August.
Brady never sent his business plan and did not answer for our call. I left a message.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

 

NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS:

 

IMPACT REMINDER:

Hi Yuki,

Thank you for the update, and great job taking initiative with the market research and getting started on your business plan I know it’s a lot to pull together. 

For your business plan, I think we should focus on a couple of things. 

  1. The SWOT analysis needs to be thought through more critically. I have a few things you could add to it.
  • Strengths
    • Overhead Costs- May be low considering you will not be doing dine in service, so no waitstaff and limited decor.
    • Tourism Traffic- Should be higher during the summer months, leading to more potential customers 
    • Convenience- Easy fast casual food allowing people to get back to their day
    • Healthy/Trendy- Japanese style food is perceived as health conscious, which could align with customer preference
  • Weaknesses
    • Seasonal Dependency- Heavy reliance on tourism during the summer could result in slow seasons during the cold months
    • Short Hou

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Good morning, it was great getting to know you and your business this morning! As promised, below is a list of financial opportunities that may be helpful. Additionally, to connect you to Rob Peterson, I will need to have you fill out this client consent form, which I have attached to this email. Once you've sent it back, I will reach out to Rob to connect you two! Thanks in advance.



1. Alice Grants

  • Industry: Any
  • Type: Resource hub for grants, loans, and financing tools
  • Eligibility: Varies (based on factors like credit score)
  • Available: 24/7, rolling basis
  • Amount: Client-dependent

2. Great Lakes Finance (SBA 504 Loans)

  • Industry: Real estate, equipment
  • Type: Loan
  • Eligibility: Business must meet SBA 504 lo

Client has created LLC and opened bank account. Spoken with Accounting Aid and taken some workshops. She is ready to create FaceBook and Instagram to begin selling to clients. Currently, staying with the Cottage Food Law requirements and when ready she will consider a commercial kitchen.

We discussed the labeling requiremnets and the tracking of info., Insurance and resources. I will send spreadsheets and links to MSU Extension and MDAR.

Good morning Caden! It was great talking with you and getting to know your business. I attached the social media plan we went over and as mentioned, the examples are geared towards a clothing boutique so keep in mind how the items would apply to you and your business.


I will talk to you next week to go over how this may look for both the businesses and consumers side!


Best,

Hi Jesus,

Thank you for meeting with me last week and sharing your vision for launching a sports academy and facility in Battle Creek by Fall 2027/2028. It was great to hear about your passion and the potential partnership with Jonah.

As we discussed, here are a few key points and next steps:

  • Since you don’t yet have an LLC, starting with the business plan template will help you clearly lay out your vision and determine the best structure for moving forward.
  • With a credit score around 685, you're in a good position to start thinking about financial planning and projections.
  • I’m attaching local industry data for sports training services in Battle Creek to help you better understand the market and identify trends and opportunities.

Please let me know how things are progressing. If you have questions or want to meet again to go over your business plan or next steps, feel free to book a time with me below:

I met with Destiny Hubbard-Summers last week and she has been selling food items without a license for awhile now. She is now moving to the Fort Worth area in July, so I recommended the following:

In order to establish a legitimate business, please do the following:

  1. Register your business as a "Doing Business As" or DBA with Fort Worth County Business Registrations.
  2. File for a State of Texas Food License and a City of Fort Worth Vendor License if you're going to be selling at gas stations, etc.
  3. Apply for business insurance, i.e. Hiscox. If www.hiscox.com or check with an insurance broker for rates.
  4. Look into entrepreneurial classes in Fort Worth Texas i.e. Community College or other small business programs. I think you could benefit from them.

I wished her good luck and if there are questions to reach out to me.

Hello S

Jeff and Brittney purchased their business 18 months ago and are focused on growing it from $300K to $500K by year end.


I met them at the office where they gave me a tour of the operations, introduced the team and showed me sample products. Today we reviewed mission and vision, ideal customer and financial overview. The business struggles with cash flow, typically due to protracted business payment terms with their larger clients. Cummins is the biggest contract, they are not fully integrated but it generates about 200K per annum, but their average client base generates between $250-$10K per job. There are some clients that they haven't tapped into, that we need to build a sales plan around. They currently don't track numbers, although they are using QuickBooks, the other issue is that Brit doesn't pay her self a normal salary, she takes approximately a 15% draw on each job, so depending on the size of a job, she can make as little as $37 to effec

Great. I apprec

Reviewed 1 year of bank statements, put into ProfitCents to assess and project new loan debt. Prep time is reviewing statements, entering into excel and LivePlan, texting Tim for clarification items. Next steps: review with Tim and discuss margins. COGS is 37.5% and wages 16%. COGS could be area of improvements. Need to ask about inventory monitoring, control. Pours, cash sales, shrink, pricing, etc. AI assisted:


Profit & Loss (P&L) Observations from Bank Statements

While bank statements aren't a formal P&L, they allow us to infer trends in revenue and expenses. A true P&L would match revenue and expenses for a specific period, regardless of when cash is received or paid. Since we only have cash transactions, we are looking at a cash-basis approximation.


Revenue (Deposits):


We noted a positive trend in deposits from August 2024 ($5,925.20) to May 2025 ($15,744.28), with a peak in April 2025 ($22,215.73). Th
I resent the attribution request.
The request is now complete.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
The discussion centered on Ashley Alleshouse's business structure and her preparations for launching her Etsy shop. Ashley confirmed her status as a disregarded entity LLC, which simplifies her tax filing process, but expressed concerns about the implications of this designation on her business identity. Kelly West emphasized the importance of maintaining a separate business bank account to protect personal and business liabilities. They also reviewed Ashley's progress in setting up her Etsy shop, with Kelly reassuring her about the legitimacy of her LLC status based on the documentation she received.

Kelly introduced a new website that requires users to create new accounts, which Ashley had not yet accessed. They navigated the site together, addressing Ashley's confusion regarding the login process and retired links. The conversation also touched on the current state of their businesses, with Ashley noting her shop remains steady as she finalizes her setup. She ex

Analyzed and reviewed Tim's hours compared to traffic counts. Suggestion would be to move hours to capture more traffic, considering his bar/Hancock as a pass-through area instead of a destination.


Traffic Counts for US 41 by your shop - thoughts on hours open


Daniel Yoder <dayoder@mtu.edu>

2:33 PM (0 minutes ago)

to Wolven, Timothy, Kevin


Hi Tim,


I pulled the most recent traffic study from July 2024 on the street by your front door. This shows the car volume (number of cars) passing by each 15 minute increment. On any given day, 5,000-7,000 cars pass by your shop.


Being open for 8 hours from 6pm to 2am gives you exposure to 1,595 cars. Being open for 8 hours from 2pm to 10pm gives you exposure to 3,549 cars.

Visited client site, met with Tim, Paulina, and head bartender Philip. Discussed ways to rebrand and better promote WolenWood. Discussed the need to track KPIs and prime costs. Although the bar is located in an ideal traffic spot, they feel it will be difficult to attract customers from passing traffic, especially within their 21-40 age range. However, the location is an ideal "billboard" to advertise the bar. Another related concern is the 6-9 pm time slot with very few customers. Offering basic pub food may help in this regard.

Also, discussed new promotional ideas.

Next steps: Time wants to discuss financial aspects with Daniel. Need to track that suggested improvements to website and socail media have been made.

I met with Tonisha Allen today to discuss her disposable bag product line. She is interested in applying for the Accelerator Course with Lawrence Tech, who will fund the prototype after completion. It is a 12 week course. Detroit Means Business funded $25k towards the prototype but ran out of funds. The Accelerator program is not a fit and she should apply for the Design for Michigan Manufacturing program. Per our conversation today, I recommend the following:

  1. We will focus on updating the pitch deck for the Design for Michigan Manufacturing application. Please send me the latest version of the pitch deck for review by Thursday, June 26th.
  2. Attached is the forecast builder and competitive analysis format. Please use these examples for your Sales Forecast and evaluating the competition. The Competitive Analysis is retail based and every question does not apply.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Hi David, 


Great speaking with you yesterday. You have made a lot of progress and there is more work ahead. We want to focus your launch on your primary target consumer. Try to consider the easiest sale based on who is buying and where you find them. It is very difficult (expensive) to chase multiple target consumers in the beginning. You have to plan adding additional target markets as you achieve growth. 

I have attached several templates that can allow you to project sales, track marketing efforts & costs, manage expenses and better understand your business. If you have any questions please let me know.


Accounting Aid Society - https://accountingaidsociety.org/

There is a Ferndale office or you can meet with them virtually.


Diana

Jessica was a no show today. BUT, I did a lot of prework for the meeting. Here is some details on the probable valuation.


Factors:

Quality of the book of business

  • Retention of clients
  • Client Concentration – diversified
  • Carrier concentration – diversified
  • Policy mix – commercial and personal

Operational efficiency

Growth potential

Location and market conditions

Staff going to stay?

Hub and spoke


Based on recent data, average revenue multiples for sold insurance agencies generally fall between 1.5x and 2.5x.


Applying this to $1,500,000 in revenue:

Low End (1.5x): $1,500,000 * 1.5 = $2,250,000

High End (2.5x - 3.5x for high-performing): $1,500,000 * 2.5 = $3,750,000 to $1,500,000 * 3.5 = $5,250,000

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Client provides a style of micro blading eyebrow work. She has been working out of her home doing about $5000 in profit a year. She wants to grow her business and has an opportunity to offer services out of a salon. She also would like to eventually have her own place. Each full client takes 3 hours and she charges $320 -$400. She can only provide service for 2 full clients and 1-2 maint. clients a day max.

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

She currently is not in compliance with the health dept and has no insurance.Those are priority next steps. She also has no accurate cost per client or keeps track of monthy expenses. That will be our goal for gathering the data. <

Initial propose for the call, they were looking at buying a flower shop that has been in existence 37 years. The sellers wanted $190,000 for the bldg, and all equipment; however, the last 5-years of profits weren't good, current clients are primarily funeral homes. Anna and Adam couldn't tell me the exact profit or loss of the business, only that they wondered how the owners were able to live on the net income.


We talked about possible financing of another greenhouse in the future, this cost all in could be about $21,000 - $25,000; including inventory. They wanted to know about loan options and we discussed micro loans with a CDFI being the best lending partner with them being in business less than 2 - 3 years. I

Our client emailed me regarding a solicitation she received in the mail. It is one that I have seen before that states that they haven't filed their Annual Statement for this year. I was able to look it up under the new MiBusiness Portal and I shared with our client that they had filed their annual statement on time.


A further complication was that she called the MTO customer service line and they told her that her company was not registered. Of course it is a separate system that will now share the same access point and they will need to set it up prior to filing next year's annual statement. I sent her the links and other information in this email: https://michigansbdcatgrandvalleystateuniv.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/EmailMessage/02sVs00000UUCfCIAX/view

Good morning, Loren, it was great connecting with you to go over some potential funding opportunities and startup cost preparation. As promised, I have attached an Excel sheet template to walk you through calculating your startups costs, it includes some example items in blue text that may or may not be applicable. Additionally, below is a brief description of the grants we went over. Let me know if you have any questions!


1. Manufacturing Growth Alliance (MGA) – Industry 4.0 Grant

  • Industry: Manufacturing
  • Eligibility: Michigan-based manufacturer, must complete a tech assessment and be a (free) member of Automation Alley
  • Type: Grant
  • Details: 50% reimbursement for qualifying tech costs (automation, robotics, etc.)
  • Amount: Up to $25,000
  • Availability: Year-round

Hi Ryan,

I wanted to provide some clarification on the financial projections I created for your business.

Since the historical financial data was provided on a quarterly basis, I used the same numbers within each respective quarter. For example, all months within Q2 2025 reflect the same figures, and this approach continues throughout each quarter.

To project future performance, I used a blended method that considered three key factors:

  • Year-over-Year average change based on historical data
  • Quarter-over-Quarter average change based on historical data
  • National industry forecasts for full-service restaurants year by year

These three inputs helped create a reasonable projection based on past performance and broader market expectations.

However, please note that these projections do not account for the upcoming construction planned for 2026. This is a signif
